title: Operations Executive ( Chemical Product )
description:
Key Responsibilities

• Assist in the preparation, review and negotiation of chemical commodity contracts, and

coordinate with counterparties on commercial and execution-related terms.

• Ensure that all commercial and operational obligations arising from contracts, including

shipment arrangements, delivery instructions and nominations, are executed accurately

and in compliance with company standards.

• Manage end-to-end logistics and vessel operations for chemical products, covering both

containerized shipments and dry bulk vessels, including vessel selection (for bulk

shipments), container booking, stowage coordination, voyage planning and execution.

• Prepare, review and coordinate shipping and trade documentation for chemical cargoes,

including but not limited to Bills of Lading, packing lists, commercial invoices, certificates

of origin, inspection certificates and MSDS where applicable.

• Act as a key operational communication interface between the company, suppliers and

end customers, ensuring timely and accurate information flow.

• Without involvement in pricing negotiations or sales decision-making, support

operational coordination with suppliers and end customers on contract execution,

shipment arrangements, delivery and documentation matters, ensuring timely issue

resolution. Due to cross-regional and time-zone differences, occasional communication

and response outside normal working hours may be required.

• Liaise closely with suppliers, shipping lines, shipbrokers, port agents, freight forwarders

and surveyors to ensure smooth and timely shipment execution.

• Monitor vessel movements and container shipment status to ensure on-time delivery to

designated receivers.

• Handle trade finance matters, including but not limited to Letters of Credit (LCs).

• Manage logistics- and trade-related claims, including bulk vessel demurrage, container

detention and demurrage, as well as claims arising from shipment, documentation or

execution issues.

• Prepare pricing and logistics cost calculations, issue invoices and monitor payment

collections.

• Ensure that all operational, logistics and trading information is entered accurately and in

a timely manner into the company’s trading and operational systems.

• Prepare and maintain carbon and sustainability-related documentation, including

support for audits related to low-carbon or renewable initiatives, such as GHG mass

balance calculations.

• Provide support for ad-hoc tasks and projects as assigned by management.

Requirements

• Minimum 2 years of experience in commodity trading operations and/or containerized and

dry bulk shipping operations, preferably with exposure to base chemicals or bulk

chemical trading.

• Subject to demonstrated professionalism and accountability, candidates with strong

growth potential, learning capability and an entrepreneurial mindset may be assessed

flexibly with regard to experience requirements.

• Proficient in Microsoft Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), with strong data

organization and cost analysis skills.

• Strong communication and coordination skills, with the ability to work effectively with

suppliers, end customers and internal stakeholders.

• Demonstrates sound judgment and discretion in handling confidential and commercially

sensitive information.

• Highly organized, detail-oriented and able to manage multiple tasks effectively in a fast-

paced environment.

• Reliable, professional and responsible, with the ability to remain effective during peak

operational periods.

• Proactive with a positive learning attitude, able to work independently as well as

collaboratively within a team.

postedCompany.description:
Do you still think the boutique agency cannot advanced your own tech recruitment career?

Are you considering to change your career path from big agency to enjoy the best commission structure and working with best in class tech clients in Singapore?

Join US!

We are the Singapore and China based technology recruitment agency and specialised in providing talent solution for internet and technology industry. Our client portfolio are including the global internet technology giants, technology MNC and Tech startup. Whatever technology clients you are think of? Yes we do have!

Due to confidence and client demand in Singapore, we're looking for an experienced tech recruitment Consultant to join our team to service our clients across Asia Pacific.

In this role you'll help our clients identify & hire talent people not only with the right skills and experience, but also the right motivational and cultural fit - people who'll stay and perform.
